title: atriplex hastata subsp. littoralis
intro: Atriplex hastata subsp. littoralis, also known as sea saltbush, is a subspecies of shrub that belongs to the amaranth family and is native to the coastal regions of Australia. The medicinal properties of Atriplex hastata subsp. littoralis include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce blood pressure, and provide antioxidant properties. Atriplex hastata subsp. littoralis can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
